programmes.show_description: British Railways issues Mr Parkin with a van for his job, but he needs to learn how to drive it. S2 E4: The Van
Gary Sparrow is an unhappily married TV repairman who one day walks into a time warp. Nicholas Lyndhurst stars in the hit 1990s sitcom. S1 E1: Rites of Passage
Mr. Bean turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment as he visits a department store and gets carried away. E2: The Return of Mr. Bean
Sam encounters young Tony Crane, a villain he put away in 2006. He realises he is faced with the chance every copper dreams about - stopping the killer before he kills. Starring John Simm. S2 E1
Sandra needs the flat to entertain her new boyfriend Danny, meaning that Beryl pretends to be with her boyfriend but is visiting Gerry in the flat downstairs instead. S2 E4: The Proposal
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
With a head full of compassion, Brenda has ideas about making Pamela feel more appreciated by her lover Sidney Clough. S1 E6: Repenting
Dorien's book group is convening to discuss Crime and Punishment, which turns out to be rather applicable to the events that unfold in Tracey's household. S11 E5: Tracey's Choice
More hilarious mishaps with the bumbling Mr. Bean, this time as he books into a hotel room and makes himself well and truly at home but locks himself out. E8: Mr. Bean in Room 426
David goes to a casino where he meets the Cochrans and hopes that Linda will bring him luck as he puts his winning system into play. S2 E2: Casino

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E3: When You've Got To Go
When a young English couple arrive at the hotel, Amy and Linda assume that they are newlyweds because they are so evidently in love. S2 E3: Couples
Teddy causes a mass walk-out at the factory, the Prime Minister is coming to dinner, and Alf is consumed by thoughts of Lady Lavender - or rather her money. S2 E2: Trouble at the Mill
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
Tofkin is back sniffing around the pub and appears to have Wolfie and the boys by the short 'n' curlies, but they're not who his eyes are set on. S3 E4: Tofkin's Revenge
Freddie is arrested for stealing a dustcart, Grandad is taken to hospital, Billy quarrels with Julie and Jack is nearly seduced by Carmen. S3 E7

There is talk of a wildcat strike among drivers - if any Hatley staff join it, the station could be closed. S2 E5: Lucky Strike

While the flatmates compete to get the flat to themselves to entertain friends, George is trying to find jobs to avoid Mildred. S5 E4: Love and Let Love

Gary returns to the bar to begin courting Phoebe, where he runs into her husband Donald. S1 E2: Fools Rush In
Mr. Bean goes to a swimming pool but struggles with the diving board, attempts to leave a car park in style and demonstrates some sandwich-making. E3: The Curse of Mr. Bean
Armed robberies lead the CID unit to prison, to escort safe-cracker Dickie Fingers for interview. They are set upon by a gang determined to get their hands on Fingers. Starring John Simm. S2 E2
Sandra and Beryl stay downstairs in Gerry's flat while he is away to help Sandra and her new husband get some time alone, but Gerry comes back early with his mum. S2 E5: The Good Samaritans

Brenda and Malcolm go for a spot of bird watching in Malcolm's hide, but Malcolm ends up flat on his back when the hide collapses; meanwhile, Oswald appears again in his cafe. S1 E7: Hiding
Tracey is out of prison, and Sharon gets a job as a school dinner supervisor and recruits Garth as her cook and Tracey as her washer-up. S11 E6: The Chief, The Cook, His Mum and Her Lodger
Mr. Bean loses midnight on New Year's Eve, devises an unusual method of transporting his sales shopping home, and wreaks havoc by doing a spot of decorating. E9: Do-It-Yourself, Mr. Bean

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E4: When Love Walks In
Linda is delighted when her heartthrob - soap actor Frazer Hines - books into the hotel, but he only has eyes for Amy. S2 E4: El Astro
The Bishop is collecting for his charity auction, Lady Lavender throws her money out of the window, and light-fingered Alf - resourceful as ever - has a plan. S2 E3 Money Talks
An election polarises the household - Rigsby supports the Conservatives, Alan and Philip lean towards Labour, and Miss Jones is impressed with the Liberal candidate. S1 E7: Stand Up and Be Counted
When Dad throws Wolfie and Ken out from their home, they sneak back in later as squatters. S3 E5: We Shall Not Be Moved

When Mildred enters a flower arranging competition, Chrissy offers her a plant from her garden; when Larry sees one in Chrissy's flat, he thinks it's a cannabis plant. S5 E5: How Does Your Garden Grow
Mr Jeremy Brown, an English teacher, meets his class of foreign students for the first time. Things start off well, but... S1 E1: The First Lesson
